original_hypothesis.title: Artist Signature Lock Bypassed by updateCollectionInfo
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The `artistSignature` function is intended to lock the collection's core identity by setting `artistSigned = true`. However, `updateCollectionInfo` only checks `collectionFreeze`, not `artistSigned`. An admin can change the collection's name, description, and generative scripts after the artist has signed it, violating the trust assumption of the signature.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The documentation states 'Only artists can sign their collections' implying the signature finalizes the art. If the admin can change the scripts post-signature, the signature provides no guarantee of immutability to buyers.

initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: Admin can modify scripts after artist approval

analysis_questions: Can the admin rug-pull the art after signature?
validation_requirements: Sign a collection as an artist
Call `updateCollectionInfo` as admin and verify scripts can be changed

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: An artist signs a collection, verifying the generative scripts are correct. The admin then maliciously alters the scripts via `updateCollectionInfo` before freezing the collection, selling altered art under the artist's signature.
prerequisites: Admin acts maliciously or is compromised

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Reputational damage and potential loss of value for buyers

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: Missing tests verifying state locks post-signature

validation_result.impact_summary: After an artist signs a collection (setting artistSigned=true), a collection/function/global admin can still modify the collection's generative scripts, name, description, library, and other metadata via updateCollectionInfo, which only checks collectionFreeze, not artistSigned.
validation_result.detailed_analysis: The artistSignature function (NextGenCore.sol) sets artistSigned[_collectionID] = true as a one-way transition intended to represent the artist's approval of the collection state. The setCollectionData function correctly respects this flag by restricting what fields can be modified once artistSigned is true. However, updateCollectionInfo does NOT check artistSigned at all — its require statement only checks isCollectionCreated and collectionFreeze. This means a collection admin can alter the generative scripts, collection name, description, website, license, library, and base URI after the artist has signed, violating the trust assumption that the artist's signature certifies the collection's content integrity. The only protection against such modification is the separate collectionFreeze mechanism, which must be explicitly activated by a function admin and is independent of the artist signature workflow.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: The defect is clearly visible by comparing updateCollectionInfo's guard (only checks collectionFreeze) with setCollectionData's guard (checks both collectionFreeze AND artistSigned). The inconsistency demonstrates that artistSigned was intended to restrict modifications but was not applied to updateCollectionInfo.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: The require statement only checks collectionFreeze, missing a check for artistSigned[_collectionID] == false. This allows full modification of collection info including generative scripts after the artist has signed.
This function correctly respects artistSigned by restricting modifiable fields once signed. The contrast with updateCollectionInfo confirms the missing check is a defect.
Sets artistSigned to true as a one-way transition, intended to lock collection identity. But this lock is not enforced by updateCollectionInfo.

calling_sequence: 1. Artist calls artistSignature(_collectionID, _signature) — sets artistSigned[_collectionID] = true
2. Collection admin (or global/function admin) calls updateCollectionInfo(_collectionID, ..., 1000, maliciousScript)
3. updateCollectionInfo checks: require(isCollectionCreated[_collectionID] == true && collectionFreeze[_collectionID] == false) — passes since only freeze is checked
5. All minted tokens' generative output changes since retrieveGenerativeScript reads from collectionInfo[].collectionScript
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: The artist signs the collection via artistSignature (line 257-262), setting artistSigned[_collectionID] = true. Subsequently, a collection admin calls updateCollectionInfo (line 238). The require on line 239 only checks collectionFreeze[_collectionID] == false, not artistSigned[_collectionID]. When _index == 1000 (line 240), all collection metadata fields including collectionScript are overwritten (lines 241-248). The generative scripts read by retrieveGenerativeScript (line 343) and tokenURI (line 313) now reflect the modified scripts, not the scripts the artist approved.

config_validation.deployment_evidence: No deployment scripts provided that are relevant to this issue. The defect exists in the core contract logic regardless of deployment configuration.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: The attack scenario is valid and realistic: (1) Artist reviews generative scripts and signs the collection via artistSignature. (2) A compromised or malicious collection admin (or global/function admin) calls updateCollectionInfo with _index=1000, replacing the generative scripts with different code. (3) Collectors who relied on the artist's signature as assurance of the art's authenticity are deceived. (4) The tokenURI and retrieveGenerativeScript functions serve the altered scripts. (5) The collection can then be frozen to make the malicious changes permanent. This attack window exists between artist signing and collection freezing, and only requires CollectionAdmin-level access.
validation_result.impact_assessment: Medium severity. The artist signature mechanism provides a false sense of security — it restricts setCollectionData but not updateCollectionInfo. A collection admin can alter the core artistic content (generative scripts, name, description, library) that the artist endorsed. This affects: (1) Artist reputation — art is changed under their signed name. (2) Collector trust — tokens may render different art than what was signed. (3) Market integrity — secondary market participants may value tokens based on the artist's signature guarantee. The impact is limited to scenarios involving malicious or compromised admins, which is a realistic threat given the multi-tier admin structure.
recommended_mitigations: Add `require(artistSigned[_collectionID] == false, "Artist has signed")` to the updateCollectionInfo function, preventing any modifications after the artist has signed
Alternatively, restrict updateCollectionInfo to only allow non-critical field changes (e.g., website URL) when artistSigned is true, similar to how setCollectionData handles it
Consider automatically freezing the collection when the artist signs, or requiring the artist to explicitly approve any subsequent changes to scripts
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy NextGenAdmins, NextGenCore, RandomizerNXT, and MinterContract. 2. Create a collection with generative scripts via createCollection. 3. Set collection data via setCollectionData with an artist address. 4. As the artist, call artistSignature to sign the collection. 5. Verify artistSigned[collectionID] == true. 6. As a collection admin, call updateCollectionInfo with _index=1000 and modified scripts. 7. Verify the call succeeds (no revert). 8. Call retrieveCollectionLibraryAndScript to confirm scripts were changed post-signature.
uncertainties: Whether the protocol designers intended artistSigned to only restrict setCollectionData and not updateCollectionInfo (design intent vs. implementation bug)
Whether the collection admin for a given collection is always the same party as the artist, which would reduce the practical impact
additional_testing_needed: Verify that all metadata-modifying functions (changeTokenData, updateImagesAndAttributes, changeMetadataView) similarly lack the artistSigned check
Test whether a collection admin registered via registerCollectionAdmin is typically the artist themselves or a separate party
